signals.headline: IMF Confirms Minimal Fed Rate Cuts in 2026 Amid Tariffs, Steady GDP Growth, and Persistent Inflation; Powell Warns Tarif
signals.raw:
IMF Confirms Minimal Fed Rate Cuts in 2026 Amid Tariffs, Steady GDP Growth, and Persistent Inflation; Powell Warns Tariffs Add 0.5-1% to Inflation** | YES | HIGH confidence

The IMF's April 2, 2026, Article IV Consultation explicitly states "little room to cut interest rates in 2026," projecting the fed funds rate easing only from 3.6% to 3.4% for the year, driven by core PCE inflation at 2.6% by
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Game 3 is scheduled for Thursday, April 23 at 8:00 PM ET in Toronto**[3], where the Raptors face elimination
signals.raw: Game 3 is scheduled for Thursday, April 23 at 8:00 PM ET in Toronto**[3], where the Raptors face elimination. The series moving to hostile territory gives Toronto a chance to regroup, but the Cavaliers' dominant two-game performance—particularly the integration of Harden into the lineup—has positioned them as strong favorites to win the series.
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: Cavaliers Lead Raptors 2-0 in First-Round Series | YES (Cavaliers favored) | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Cavaliers Lead Raptors 2-0 in First-Round Series | YES (Cavaliers favored) | HIGH confidence**

The **Cavaliers defeated the Raptors 115-105 on Monday, April 21**, securing a 2-0 series lead in their Eastern Conference first-round matchup[1][3]. This development significantly strengthens Cleveland's position, as winning the first two games at home puts them one victory away from advancing.
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: **Evan Mobley** posted 25 points in the Game 2 victory[3]
signals.raw:
**Evan Mobley** posted 25 points in the Game 2 victory[3]

This marks the fourth trio in Cavaliers playoff history to score at this level together[1], demonstrating the team's offensive firepower. The Raptors' **Scottie Barnes** led Toronto with 26 points, but it was insufficient to overcome Cleveland's balanced attack[1].
